level_signals: Described as leading large-scale, high-impact, cross-functional R&D initiatives
Works closely with senior leadership and key stakeholders
Defines program management standards and best practices org-wide

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Extensive technical program management or similar relevant experience
Prior experience of successfully implementing and managing SaaS product development programs; experience in product management or engineering roles preferred
Solid technical foundation with ability to understand and digest new technical concepts and technologies quickly
Experience in practicing Agile, with the ability to adjust effectively based on current needs and team dynamics
Strong experience with program management tools; proficiency in Jira required
Strong influencing and negotiating skills at all levels
Comfortable adjusting between the need to be tactical vs. strategic
Expert in navigating through, and working with ambiguity by bringing structure to large/complex problems
Organized and detail oriented; comfortable managing multiple initiatives and priorities in parallel
Applicants must have, and maintain, the right to work in the United Kingdom from the first day of employment. Please note that visa sponsorship is not available for this role.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: experience in product management or engineering roles preferred

explicitly_excluded: Visa sponsorship — not available for this role

scope.surface: R&D Operations — internal shared services for R&D organization (Product and Engineering teams); Initiative management, OKR planning, operating metrics, systems administration
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — wealth management and financial data platform
core_responsibilities: Drive a portfolio of high-priority R&D Initiatives, including the creation and management of detailed plans and artifacts

Lead strategic and tactical weekly cross-functional technical delivery forums across Product, Engineering, Risk and other key stakeholders, as well as monthly Steering Committee forums (SteerCos) with senior leadership
Develop strong partnerships with cross-functional teams to drive progress, facilitate collaboration, and remove obstacles
Identify program risks and establish escalation process for facilitating 'Return to Green' plan for initiatives that are off-track
Flex technical expertise and emotional intelligence to align discussions to business outcomes; influence strategy and trade-off decisions to optimize impact and time-to-market
Coordinate, design, and implement improvements to the quarterly R&D-wide Planning process
Establish program management standards and best practices, including systems and tools
Standardize JIRA use and operating metrics across teams to drive consistent telemetry, while building velocity measuring tools to track initiative and OKR progress
Manage user permissions to R&D Ops-managed systems

green_flags: Clear ownership scope — defining 'how we program manage' at Addepar org-wide
Explicitly described team culture values: ownership, transparency, collaboration, creativity
Role sits at a high-impact level with direct access to senior leadership via monthly SteerCos
Company manages ~$9T in assets with 1,400+ clients — stable, scaled enterprise
red_flags: Visa sponsorship explicitly unavailable — requires existing UK right to work, limiting candidate pool
No compensation or equity information disclosed
Years of experience not quantified — 'extensive' is vague and may exclude candidates who don't self-select in
Location specifics (city within UK) not stated — office expectations unclear beyond right to work in UK

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff' title and scope of responsibilities; no years stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — B2B SaaS, wealth management platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite; JD requires UK right to work but does not explicitly state remote/hybrid/onsite — treated as onsite given no remote language)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity anywhere in JD)
ambiguities: Location type is unclear — JD requires UK right to work and lists UK offices (London, Edinburgh) but does not explicitly state whether the role is remote, hybrid, or fully onsite within the UK. Treated as onsite given absence of remote language.
'Extensive technical program management' experience is undefined in years — severity treated as hard per unconditional phrasing but exact threshold is ambiguous.

SaaS product development experience is listed as a standalone bullet (implying hard requirement) but the preferred clause ('experience in product management or engineering roles preferred') within the same bullet suggests partial softness — the SaaS program experience is treated as hard, PM/engineering background as soft.
